001package guiFirstAdmin;
002
003import java.sql.SQLException;
004import applicationMain.ValidationUtil;
005import database.Database;
006import entityClasses.User;
007import javafx.stage.Stage;
008
009/*******
010 * <p> Title: ControllerFirstAdmin Class. </p>
011 * 
012 * <p> Description: ControllerFirstAdmin class provides the controller actions based on the user's
013 *  use of the JavaFX GUI widgets defined by the View class.
014 * 
015 * This page contains a number of buttons that have not yet been implemented.  WHhen those buttons
016 * are pressed, an alert pops up to tell the user that the function associated with the button has
017 * not been implemented. Also, be aware that What has been implemented may not work the way the
018 * final product requires and there maybe defects in this code.
019 * 
020 * The class has been written assuming that the View or the Model are the only class methods that
021 * can invoke these methods.  This is why each has been declared at "protected".  Do not change any
022 * of these methods to public.</p>
023 * 
024 * <p> Copyright: Lynn Robert Carter © 2025 </p>
025 * 
026 * @author Lynn Robert Carter
027 * 
028 * @version 1.00                2025-08-17 Initial version
029 *  
030 */
031
032public class ControllerFirstAdmin {
033        /*-********************************************************************************************
034
035        The controller attributes for this page
036        
037        This controller is not a class that gets instantiated.  Rather, it is a collection of protected
038        static methods that can be called by the View (which is a singleton instantiated object) and 
039        the Model is often just a stub, or will be a singleton instantiated object.
040        
041        */
042        
043        private static String adminUsername = "";
044        private static String adminPassword1 = "";
045        private static String adminPassword2 = "";              
046        protected static Database theDatabase = applicationMain.FoundationsMain.database;               
047
048        /*-********************************************************************************************
049
050        The User Interface Actions for this page
051        
052        */
053        
054        /**
055         * Default constructor is not used.
056         */
057        public ControllerFirstAdmin() {
058        }
059
060        /**********
061         * <p> Method: setAdminUsername() </p>
062         * 
063         * <p> Description: This method is called when the user adds text to the username field in the
064         * View.  A private local copy of what was last entered is kept here.</p>
065         * 
066         */
067        protected static void setAdminUsername() {
068                adminUsername = ViewFirstAdmin.text_AdminUsername.getText();
069        }
070        
071        
072        /**********
073         * <p> Method: setAdminPassword1() </p>
074         * 
075         * <p> Description: This method is called when the user adds text to the password 1 field in
076         * the View.  A private local copy of what was last entered is kept here.</p>
077         * 
078         */
079        protected static void setAdminPassword1() {
080                adminPassword1 = ViewFirstAdmin.text_AdminPassword1.getText();
081                ViewFirstAdmin.label_PasswordsDoNotMatch.setText("");
082        }
083        
084        
085        /**********
086         * <p> Method: setAdminPassword2() </p>
087         * 
088         * <p> Description: This method is called when the user adds text to the password 2 field in
089         * the View.  A private local copy of what was last entered is kept here.</p>
090         * 
091         */
092        protected static void setAdminPassword2() {
093                adminPassword2 = ViewFirstAdmin.text_AdminPassword2.getText();          
094                ViewFirstAdmin.label_PasswordsDoNotMatch.setText("");
095        }
096        
097        
098        /**********
099         * <p> Method: doSetupAdmin() </p>
100         * 
101         * <p> Description: This method is called when the user presses the button to set up the Admin
102         * account.  It start by trying to establish a new user and placing that user into the
103         * database.  If that is successful, we proceed to the UserUpdate page.</p>
104         * 
105         */
106        protected static void doSetupAdmin(Stage ps, int r) {
107                String username = ViewFirstAdmin.text_AdminUsername.getText();
108                String password1 = ViewFirstAdmin.text_AdminPassword1.getText();
109                String password2 = ViewFirstAdmin.text_AdminPassword2.getText();
110                
111                String usernameError = ValidationUtil.validateAsuUserId(username);
112                if (usernameError != null) {
113                        ViewFirstAdmin.alertUsernamePasswordError.setContentText(usernameError);
114                        ViewFirstAdmin.alertUsernamePasswordError.showAndWait();
115                        return;
116                }
117                
118                String passwordError = ValidationUtil.validatePassword(password1);
119                if (passwordError != null) {
120                        ViewFirstAdmin.alertUsernamePasswordError.setContentText(passwordError);
121                        ViewFirstAdmin.alertUsernamePasswordError.showAndWait();
122                        return;
123                }
124                
125                if (password2 == null || password2.isEmpty()) {
126                        ViewFirstAdmin.alertUsernamePasswordError.setContentText("Password is required.");
127                        ViewFirstAdmin.alertUsernamePasswordError.showAndWait();
128                        return;
129                }
130                
131                // Make sure the two passwords are the same
132                if (password1.compareTo(password2) == 0) {
133                // Create the passwords and proceed to the user home page
134                        User user = new User(username, password1, "", "", "", "", "", true, false, 
135                                false);
136            try {
137                // Create a new User object with admin role and register in the database
138                theDatabase.register(user);
139                }
140            catch (SQLException e) {
141                System.err.println("*** ERROR *** Database error trying to register a user: " + 
142                                e.getMessage());
143                e.printStackTrace();
144                System.exit(0);
145            }
146            
147            // User was established in the database, so navigate to the User Update Page
148                guiUserUpdate.ViewUserUpdate.displayUserUpdate(ViewFirstAdmin.theStage, user);
149                }
150                else {
151                        // The two passwords are NOT the same, so clear the passwords, explain the passwords
152                        // must be the same, and clear the message as soon as the first character is typed.
153                        ViewFirstAdmin.text_AdminPassword1.setText("");
154                        ViewFirstAdmin.text_AdminPassword2.setText("");
155                        ViewFirstAdmin.label_PasswordsDoNotMatch.setText(
156                                        "The two passwords must match. Please try again!");
157                }
158        }
159        
160        
161        /**********
162         * <p> Method: doEmailLogin() </p>
163         * 
164         * <p> Description: This method is called when the user clicks the "Sign in with Email" button.
165         * It redirects to the email registration page where users can create accounts with their email.
166         * 
167         */     
168        protected static void doEmailLogin(Stage ps) {
169                guiEmailRegister.ViewEmailRegister.displayEmailRegister(ps);
170        }
171
172        
173        /**********
174         * <p> Method: performQuit() </p>
175         * 
176         * <p> Description: This method terminates the execution of the program.  It leaves the
177         * database in a state where the normal login page will be displayed when the application is
178         * restarted.</p>
179         * 
180         */
181        protected static void performQuit() {
182                System.out.println("Perform Quit");
183                System.exit(0);
184        }       
185}
186
